Ukraine Conflict News





11 January 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • Trump Says Venezuela Now Protected by US, Not Cuba Sputnik 11 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said on Sunday that Venezuela no longer needed Cuba for protection as it would be protected by the US military.
  • Venezuelan Foreign Ministry Denies US Claims About Unsafe Situation in Country Sputnik 11 Jan 2026 -- The Venezuelan Foreign Ministry has rejected US claims of an unsafe situation in the country and the presence of armed groups at checkpoints.
  • US Energy Secretary Says US to Increase Involvement in Venezuela's Oil Sector Sputnik 11 Jan 2026 -- The role of American companies in Venezuela's oil sector will be expanded, US Energy Secretary Chris Wright said on Sunday.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Trump orders US military to draw up plan for Greenland invasion: Report Press TV 11 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has ordered American military forces to draw up a plan for the potential takeover of Greenland, according to a report.

  • Europe drawing up plan for possible NATO mission on Greenland, mulling sanction against US companies in response to US' continued threats: media Global Times 11 Jan 2026 -- The UK is in talks with European allies about deploying a military force to Greenland that would guard the Arctic region, media reported on Saturday, after US President Donald Trump claimed that the US will take Greenland the "hard way" if it can't do it the "easy way."
  • Nordics Reject Trump's Claims of Russian, Chinese Vessels Near Greenland - Reports Sputnik 11 Jan 2026 -- Nordic countries have refuted US President Donald Trump's claims about Russian and Chinese vessels operating near Greenland, which he cited to justify his plans to take control of the island, the Financial Times reported on Sunday, citing two senior Nordic diplomats with access to NATO intelligence.
  • EU Preparing Sanctions Against US Companies Over Greenland Claims - Reports Sputnik 11 Jan 2026 -- The European Union is preparing sanctions against American companies in the event that the United States does not renounce its claims to Greenland, the Telegraph reported without specifying a source.
  • NATO States Consider Arctic Troop Presence as US Eyes Greenland Sputnik 11 Jan 2026 -- The United Kingdom is holding talks with other European NATO allies about deploying NATO troops in Greenland to protect the island from annexation by the United States, The Telegraph newspaper reported.

  • The First Priority Is Air Defense Missiles. Tomorrow We Have a Meeting Where We Will Be Discussing This - Address by the President President of Ukraine 11 Jan 2026
  • UK to develop new deep strike ballistic missile for Ukraine UK MOD 11 Jan 2026 -- The UK will develop new tactical ballistic missiles that boost Ukraine's firepower to defend itself from Putin's war machine.

  • Details of upgraded J-20A, J-20S stealth aircraft unveiled by official media amid 15th anniv. of J-20's maiden flight Global Times 11 Jan 2026 -- Chinese official media unveiled more details about two upgraded versions of the J-20 stealth fighter jet, the J-20A and the J-20S, on Sunday, the 15th anniversary of the J-20's maiden flight, highlighting the continuous evolution of the J-20 series aircraft.
  • China's self-developed ton-class unmanned transport drone Tianma-1000 completes maiden flight Global Times 11 Jan 2026 -- China's domestically developed ton-class unmanned transport aircraft Tianma-1000 completed its maiden flight on Sunday, the Xinhua News Agency reported. The drone was independently developed by China North Industries Group Corp-affiliated Xi'an Aisheng Technology Group Co.

  • TPP chair leads delegation to U.S. for talks on arms procurement, tariffs CNA 11 Jan 2026 -- A delegation led by opposition Taiwan People's Party (TPP) Chairman Huang Kuo-chang (黃國昌) on Sunday left for a brief trip to Washington, D.C., where it is scheduled to meet with U.S. government officials to discuss arms procurement and tariffs.
  • Taiwan Marine Corps' new unit equipped with U.S.-made M4A1 rifles: Source CNA 11 Jan 2026 -- A newly formed unit under Taiwan's Marine Corps tasked with land-based security operations has recently replaced its aging, domestically produced rifles with more advanced, U.S.-made M4A1 rifles, a source confirmed to CNA on Saturday.

  • Pakistan Navy Flotilla comprising of PNS RAH NAWARD (Sail Ship), PNS MADADGAR and Pakistan Maritime Security Agency (PMSA) ship PMSS KASHMIR visited Port Sultan Qaboos, Muscat, Oman. ISPR Pakistan 11 Jan 2026

  • Major investment in ground-based air defence worth approximately SEK 15 billion Government Offices of Sweden 11 Jan 2026 -- The Government has decided that the Swedish Armed Forces will expand its air defence capability. The Swedish Armed Forces will produce, train and equip a number of units within a future ground-based air defence that will protect combat units, military defence mobilisation capacity, population centres and civilian infrastructure. The total investment is worth approximately SEK 15 billion.
  • Speech by Minister for Civil Defence Carl-Oskar Bohlin at the Folk och Försvar National Conference 2026 Government Offices of Sweden 11 Jan 2026

  • Statement on the fulfilment of objectives towards the Lebanese State's monopoly on arms Spain MFA 11 Jan 2026 -- The Spanish Government welcomes the statement by the Lebanese Armed Forces (LAF) on the fulfilment of the objectives of Phase I of the restoration of the State's monopoly on the use of force throughout the country.

  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Republic of Cyprus Foreign Minister Kombos US Dept. of State 11 Jan 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with French Foreign Minister Barrot US Dept. of State 11 Jan 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Mexican Foreign Secretary de la Fuente US Dept. of State 11 Jan 2026

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Israel plans to launch renewed offensive on Gaza in March: Report Press TV 11 Jan 2026 -- The Israeli regime has reportedly drawn up plans for a renewed offensive in the Gaza Strip even as the US-brokered ceasefire moves closer to the second phase.
  • Israel Plans New Offensive in Gaza in March - Reports Sputnik 11 Jan 2026 -- Israel is planning a new offensive in the Gaza Strip in March, the Times of Israel reported citing sources.

  • Israeli strikes kill two more Palestinians, injure several others in Gaza amid truce violations Press TV 11 Jan 2026 -- Israeli forces have killed two Palestinians and wounded several others in separate attacks across the Gaza Strip, in the latest violations of a ceasefire agreement between the Tel Aviv regime and the Hamas resistance movement that went into effect on October 10, 2025.

  • Israel plans to expel Gaza Palestinians to Somaliland: Somali minister Press TV 11 Jan 2026 -- Somali Defense Minister Ahmed Moalim Fiqi says Israel plans to expel the Palestinians living in the Gaza Strip and forcefully send them to Somaliland.

  • Foreign Minister MOTEGI's Visit to the Civil-Military Coordination Center (CMCC) Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 11 Jan 2026
  • Meeting between Foreign Minister MOTEGI and Dr. Mustafa, Prime Minister of Palestine Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 11 Jan 2026
  • Courtesy Call on H.E. Mr. al-Sheikh, Vice President of Palestine by Foreign Minister MOTEGI Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 11 Jan 2026

    • Other Conflicts

  • Taliban Gold Rush Turns Deadly, Putting Spotlight On Chinese-Backed Mining RFE/RL 11 Jan 2026 -- A deadly confrontation at a gold mine in northern Afghanistan has cast new light on the Taliban's push to expand gold extraction, raising questions about the largely unregulated operations and their foreign - mostly Chinese - backing.

  • Cambodian Prime Minister Hun Manet announces measures to address impacts of the border conflict, thanks China's support Global Times 11 Jan 2026 -- Cambodian Prime Minister Hun Manet on Saturday announced measures to address the impacts of the border conflict with Thailand as recovery efforts intensify, according to Cambodian media.

News Reports

  • Cuban president to Trump: We're prepared to defend Cuba 'to the last drop of blood' Press TV 11 Jan 2026 -- Cuban President Miguel Diaz-Canel has firmly rejected US President Donald Trump's threat against his country, stressing that Havana is prepared to defend itself "to the last drop of blood."
  • Cuba Ready to Defend Itself to 'Last Drop of Blood' Amid US Threats - President Sputnik 11 Jan 2026 -- Cuban President Miguel Diaz-Canel Bermudez said on Sunday that his country has been under attack by the United States for 66 years and is ready to defend itself to the last drop of blood.
  • Cuba Does Not Intervene Militarily in Other States' Affairs, Unlike US - Foreign Minister Sputnik 11 Jan 2026 -- Cuba does not do mercenarism, blackmail or military intervention in the affairs of other countries, unlike the United States, Cuban Foreign Minister Bruno Rodriguez said on Sunday.

  • Russia Adds Almost 500Mln Tonnes in Commercial Oil Reserves in 2025 - Rosnedra Sputnik 11 Jan 2026 -- The preliminary increase in commercial, or production-ready, oil reserves in 2025 was 490 million tonnes, while gas reserves will increase by 650 billion cubic meters, Oleg Kazanov, the head of Russia's Federal Agency for Mineral Resources (Rosnedra), told Sputnik.

  • Details of bribery case against former agriculture minister exposed in annual anti-graft documentary aired since Sunday Global Times 11 Jan 2026 -- An annual heavyweight anti-corruption documentary series premiered on Sunday, portraying through 12 cases, including details of the bribery case against former minister of agriculture and rural affairs, how discipline inspection agencies in China relentlessly advance the integrated fight against corruption.
  • China, Tanzania pledge to align development strategies Global Times 11 Jan 2026 -- Tanzania and China have committed to synergizing the implementation of development strategies to boost common development, according to a joint press communique released on Saturday.
  • JOINT PRESS COMMUNIQUE ON THE BILATERAL TALKS BETWEEN THE FOREIGN MINISTERS OF THE PEOPLE'S REPUBLIC OF CHINA AND THE UNITED REPUBLIC OF TANZANIA PRC MofA 11 Jan 2026 -- At the invitation of Minister for Foreign Affairs and East African Cooperation of the United Republic of Tanzania, Honorable Ambassador Mahmoud Thabit Kombo, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Minister for Foreign Affairs of the People's Republic of China, Honorable Wang Yi, undertook a visit to Tanzania from 9th to 10th January 2026.
  • Joint Press Communiqué Between the Government of the People's Republic of China and The Government of the Kingdom of Lesotho PRC MofA 11 Jan 2026 -- At the invitation of Hon. Lejone Mpotjoana, Minister of Foreign Affairs and International Relations of Lesotho, H.E. Wang Yi, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Minister of Foreign Affairs of China, paid an official visit to the Kingdom of Lesotho from January 10 to 11, 2026.
  • Joint Press Release on the Talks Between the Foreign Ministers of The People's Republic of China and The Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia PRC MofA 11 Jan 2026 -- Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Minister of Foreign Affairs of the People's Republic of China Wang Yi visited Ethiopia from January 7 to 9, 2026. During the visit, His Excellency held talks with H.E. Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed and H.E. Minister of Foreign Affairs Gedion Timothewos.
  • China, Tanzania pledge to align development strategies Global Times 11 Jan 2026 -- Tanzania and China have committed to synergizing the implementation of development strategies to boost common development, according to a joint press communique released on Saturday.
  • Japanese PM's claim on China's export control on dual-use items exposes 'lack of sincerity and dangerous foreign policy approach,' expert says Global Times 11 Jan 2026 -- Following China's decision to tighten export controls on dual-use items destined for Japan last week, Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, speaking on an NHK program aired on Sunday, criticized China's move to strengthen export controls on military-civilian dual-use items to Japan, a move that a Chinese expert said shows that the Japanese political figure's latest claims once again expose "her lack of sincerity and the dangerous nature of her approach to foreign affairs, as well as her failure to reflect on her erroneous words and actions."

  • China uses fake news sites, accounts to spread misinformation against Taiwan: NSB CNA 11 Jan 2026 -- Taiwan's top intelligence agency on Sunday said its research shows that China has been using Chinese IT and marketing companies to create fake news sites, manage accounts, and collect and spread misinformation, as part of its cognitive warfare against Taiwan.
  • Analysis of China's Cognitive Warfare Tactics Against Taiwan in 2025 ROC NSB 11 Jan 2026 -- China continues to conduct cognitive warfare against Taiwan, hyping up selected important current events in Taiwan to achieve its strategic goals of "exacerbating internal divisions within Taiwan," "weakening Taiwanese people's will to resist the enemy," "influencing allies' willingness to support Taiwan," and "winning support for China's stance." The NSB has compiled a report titled "Analysis of China's Cognitive Warfare Tactics Against Taiwan in 2025," to help the general public be aware of China's influence operations and their threats to democratic society.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 11 Jan 2026

  • Press Statement of Kim Yo Jong, Vice Department Director of C.C., WPK KCNA 11 Jan 2026 -- Kim Yo Jong, vice department director of the Central Committee of the Workers' Party of Korea, issued the following press statement "The ROK authorities can not evade their responsibility for the grave violation of our sovereignty" on January 10:
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Guides Fourth-stage Construction Project in Hwasong Area Nearing Completion KCNA 11 Jan 2026 -- Kim Jong Un , general secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea and president of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, gave field guidance over the four-stage construction project in the Hwasong area nearing completion on Jan. 10.

  • Trump Weighs Response To Iran's Harsh Crackdown On Nationwide Protests RFE/RL 11 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has reportedly been briefed on options to respond to the harsh security crackdown on protests in Iran, as anti-government demonstrations continued for a 15th straight day.
  • Trump Seriously Considering Options for Attack Against Iran - Reports Sputnik 11 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has been briefed on possible attack options against Iran amid protests in the country, and he is seriously considering approving the strikes, the New York Times reported citing officials.
  • FM: There's evidence linking deadly violence in Iran to Mossad terrorists Press TV 11 Jan 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says there is clear evidence linking the deadly violence in Iran to terrorists associated with Israel's Mossad spy agency.
  • 'Organized network' carried out Daesh‑like attacks in Iranian cities: Security chief Press TV 11 Jan 2026 -- A "destructive and organized network" infiltrated recent riots and carried out violent acts in Iranian cities resembling tactics used by Daesh terrorists, Iran's National Security Council Secretary Ali Larijani has said.
  • President Pezeshkian calls on citizens not to let 'rioters and terrorists' destabilize Iran Press TV 11 Jan 2026 -- Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian says the nation should not allow rioters to cause insecurity in the country, stressing that protesting is different from rioting.
  • Iran recognizes rightful protests but stands firmly against terrorists: Parliament speaker Press TV 11 Jan 2026 -- Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf says Iran recognizes people's peaceful protests over economic concerns, but stands firmly against armed terrorists.
  • Iran's Constitutional Council urges police to act decisively against rioters, saboteurs Press TV 11 Jan 2026 -- Iran's Constitutional Council has strongly condemned the destruction of public properties and desecration of mosques by saboteurs across the country, calling on security forces and relevant bodies to take all necessary measures to restore law and order in the face of the violence.
  • Thirty security forces killed in foreign-backed riots in Isfahan: Official Press TV 11 Jan 2026 -- Isfahan's governor confirmed that 30 security personnel have been killed during the recent foreign-backed riots in the province.
  • UNICEF calls for the protection of children and adolescents amid public unrest in Iran UNICEF 11 Jan 2026
  • UN chief 'shocked' by reports of excessive force against protesters in Iran UN News 11 Jan 2026 -- The UN Secretary-General is shocked by reports of violence and excessive use of force by Iranian authorities against protesters across the country, urging restraint and the immediate restoration of communications as unrest enters its third week.
  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on the Islamic Republic of Iran UN Secretary-General 11 Jan 2026
  • PM Netanyahu at the Start of the Government Meeting Israel - Prime Minister 11 Jan 2026 -- "Israel is closely monitoring the events unfolding in Iran."
  • The Situation in Iran (Statement by Foreign Minister MOTEGI Toshimitsu) Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 11 Jan 2026
  • Iran warns to strike back if Washington attacks; potential US strikes risk triggering higher regional tension: expert Global Times 11 Jan 2026 -- Iran warned Washington on Sunday that any US attack would lead to Tehran striking back against Israel and regional US military bases as "legitimate targets" after the US President Donald Trump has been briefed on options for military strikes in Iran amid ongoing protests in the country, according to media reports.
  • Iranian Protesters Remain On Streets As Trump, West Step Up Pressure On Tehran RFE/RL 11 Jan 2026 -- Iranian protesters showed no signs of letup as the country entered the 15th straight day of anti-government demonstrations despite a mounting crackdown by security forces and as the West, led by US President Donald Trump, intensified pressure on the hard-line rulers in Tehran.
  • Trump reportedly briefs on options for military strikes in Iran; expert warns of renewed, higher regional tension Global Times 11 Jan 2026 -- The New York Times reported Saturday local time that the US President Donald Trump has been briefed on options for military strikes in Iran amid ongoing protests in the country. On the same day, he renewed threats against Tehran by stating the US "stands ready to help" regarding the situation.

  • PM Netanyahu's Statement Israel - Prime Minister 11 Jan 2026 -- "I attach enormous importance to the overall cooperation between Israel and Germany, and especially Israel and Germany on this question of cybersecurity, which is one of the main threats to our internal security, and in many ways also our infrastructure and other threats."
  • Courtesy Call on Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel by Foreign Minister MOTEGI (Results) Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 11 Jan 2026
  • Japan-Israel Foreign Ministers' Meeting and Working Lunch Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 11 Jan 2026
  • Israeli police arrest senior aide to Netanyahu for obstructing probe tied to Gaza war leaks Press TV 11 Jan 2026 -- Israeli police have detained a senior aide to Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, suspected of obstructing an investigation allegedly tied to leaks of military information during the Gaza war.
  • Argentina cancels Tel Aviv embassy relocation over Israel's drilling in South Atlantic: Report Press TV 11 Jan 2026 -- Argentina's President Javier Milei has reportedly frozen at the last minute the relocation of the country's embassy from Tel Aviv to the occupied al-Quds.

  • Visit of External Affairs Minister to France and Luxembourg (04-09 January 2026) India MEA 11 Jan 2026 -- The External Affairs Minister of India, Dr. S. Jaishankar, paid an official visit to the Republic of France and the Grand Duchy of Luxembourg from 04-09 January 2026 reflective of India's growing engagement with Europe.

  • Statement by the Spokesperson Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 11 Jan 2026 -- Responding to media queries regarding the demarche made to the Ambassador of Norway to Pakistan over his attendance at a court case in Islamabad, the Spokesperson of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Mr. Tahir Andrabi, stated the following:

  • Strengthening the transatlantic partnership: visit by Foreign Minister Wadephul to the United States Germany Federal Foreign Office 11 Jan 2026 -- Foreign Minister Wadephul is travelling to Washington DC today for talks with US Secretary of State Rubio.
  • Statement by Foreign Minister Wadephul prior to his departure for Reykjavík, Washington, D.C. and New York Germany Federal Foreign Office 11 Jan 2026
